title: Little Movers Guide - Home
body: Welcome to the Little Movers draft space. Six chapters co-written by Greg Howard DPT and Sarah Mitchell DPT for parents of children with developmental delays.

title: Chapter 1 - Understanding Your Child's Movement
body: Chapter 1 draft: foundational concepts around gross motor development for ages 0-5. Covers typical milestones, red flags, and when to seek PT evaluation.

title: Chapter 2 - Safe Floor Play and Tummy Time
body: Chapter 2 draft: guided tummy time routines, supported sitting progressions, and safe play environment setup for infants and toddlers.

title: Chapter 3 - Standing and Early Walking
body: Chapter 3 draft: supported standing exercises, cruising activities along furniture, and pre-walking strengthening activities.

title: Chapter 4 - Balance and Coordination Activities
body: Chapter 4 draft: age-appropriate balance challenges, coordination games, and proprioceptive activities for children 2-5.

title: Chapter 5 - Sensory Play and Body Awareness
body: Chapter 5 draft (early): sensory integration basics for parents, texture play, proprioceptive input activities, and calming strategies.

title: Chapter 6 - When to Call Your PT (and What to Expect)
body: Chapter 6 draft: navigating referrals, what a pediatric PT evaluation looks like, questions to ask, and how to carry over clinic exercises at home.

title: Style Guide and Formatting Standards
body: Tone: warm, plain language, no clinical jargon without definition. Illustrations for every exercise. Each activity needs: goal, setup, step-by-step, tips, and safety note.

title: Beta Reader Feedback - Round 1
body: Feedback from first beta readers: Rachel Torres (expectant parent), two Kestrel parent volunteers. Key themes: language accessibility, photo quality needs, chapter 2 most helpful.

title: Illustration Brief for Designer
body: Brief for contract illustrator: warm, diverse families, simple line-art style. Avoid clinical imagery. Each chapter needs 6-8 illustrations. Budget $1,200 total.

title: CNA Home
body: Clintonville Neighborhood Association shared docs - meeting minutes, event plans, committee notes.

title: Fall Festival 2026 Planning Notes
body: Master planning document for Clintonville Fall Festival Oct 17 2026. Venue: Whetstone Park. Budget: $4,800. Expected attendance: 600-800.

title: Spring Cleanup 2026 - After-Action Report
body: Mar 14 2026 cleanup day recap: 25 volunteers, 6 zones, 42 bags collected. Photos archived in Google Drive. Suggestions for fall event included.

title: Board Meeting Minutes - March 2026 (Spring Planning)
body: Mar 23 2026 board meeting minutes. Attendees: Greg Howard, Marcus Webb, Beth Kowalski, Priya Subramaniam, Pastor Reardon. Topics: fall festival vendors, cleanup day planning, budget review.

title: Volunteer Onboarding Guide
body: Welcome to volunteering with the Clintonville Neighborhood Association! This guide covers role descriptions, contact lists, tools we use, and how to sign up for events.

title: Fall Festival 2025 - After-Action Report
body: Oct 18 2025 festival recap: 650 attendees, 7 vendors, 22 volunteers, $3,400 raised for neighborhood beautification fund. Weather: perfect. Lessons learned for 2026 included.

title: 2026 Event Calendar
body: Key 2026 dates: Mar 14 Spring Cleanup; Mar 28 Plot Assignments Open; Oct 11 Fall Cleanup; Oct 17 Fall Festival; Nov 22 Church Community Meal; Dec holiday lighting date pending.

title: Whetstone Park Improvement Proposals
body: Proposals submitted to Columbus Rec & Parks: resurfaced walking trail (north loop), two additional park benches near pavilion, updated picnic tables. Budget request: $8,500.

title: Board Meeting Minutes - March 2026
body: Mar 9 2026 board meeting. Topics: spring cleanup logistics, festival permit timeline, newsletter editorial calendar, block captain network updates.

title: Sponsor Package 2026
body: Sponsorship tiers for Fall Festival 2026: Bronze $250 (logo in program), Silver $500 (banner + logo), Gold $1,000 (stage mention + banner + social), Presenting $2,500 (all + dedicated booth).
